logs.name: internal-oak
logs.text:
I can understand this logic for other Republican politicians I've disliked, such as George W. Bush. I used to hate Bush, but I don't think I would have ever argued that he didn't generally mean well.

Trump, to me, is in a different category, where supporting him crosses over from holding one's nose at a non-ideal candidate to supporting someone who is actively immoral and thereby compromising your own morals. I can even understand someone voting for him because of the poor choices, but not with them still supporting him or the party at this point.

To support someone who is immoral because one thinks it will advance their interests, is to me by extension inherently immoral. The anti-intellectualism also stops me from buying that. Trump is objectively proven to be a liar on many important issues. How is supporting that OK in any context?

The last part, about someone who stands by the party because of the idea of what it should or used to stand for, rather than what it currently does, is the only piece that I can buy. But is that really a significant subset of the Republican party?

logs.name: raw-armament
logs.text:
In this election specifically, it looks like many Republicans wished to select the lesser of two evils in their mind: Trump or Hillary. You can't really group all Republican voters into the Trump camp becuase fewer than 50% even voted for him in most of the early primaries. So when Trump wins the Republican nomination, all the Republicans who are  of Trump supporters must discern who they should vote for, a Republican candidate who they don't particularly like, or a Democrat they *really* don't like. When you put it this way, it's easy to see why Republican voters would choose Trump. Most Republicans value lower taxes and more deregulation of the economy, so they vote for Trump on the basis of "at least he will do something I like."

The same goes for state Senators and Reps. To most Republicans, the Republican will do the least harm and the most good. Additionally, a lot of people on both sides are largely ignorant of what their Senators and Representatives actually say or do, so it is unfair to say that Republicans are ignorant and Democrats are not. Are there climate change denying, racists, bigoted Republicans out there? Sure, but they really aren't that prevalent. Most Republicans simply vote for climate change deniers becuase they share other values and ideals, not becuase they are climate change deniers. I have one Republican friend who, while staunchly conservative, is very concerned about the environment. However, he will always vote Republican because he believes in the principles of a free market, less regulation (in general), and conservative moral values.

logs.name: yummy-dam
logs.text: "I completely disagree with raw-armament's assessment. Just because people vote for Trump because they think he's the lesser of two evils doesn't mean they're ignorant or that their values align with his. It's a cop-out to say that Republicans are just voting for the 'least harm' or'most good'. That's a simplistic view that ignores the fact that Trump's policies are actively harming the very people he claims to be helping. And let's be real, most Republicans aren't as concerned about the environment as they claim to be. They're just using it as a talking point to sound good. I'm not buying it."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the discussion respectful and fact-based. @yummy-dam, @raw-armament, your points are noted. Let's avoid generalizations and stick to constructive dialogue. Remember, the goal is to understand different perspectives, not to dismiss them."

logs.name: pounded-pool
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @yummy-dam? You think you're so smart with your 'constructive dialogue' and 'fact-based' nonsense. Newsflash: most people don't care about facts, they care about what makes them feel good. And if that means voting for Trump because he's the lesser of two evils, so be it. Don't try to lecture me about the environment, I'm not buying it. And by the way, @moderator, what's with the constant reminders to be'respectful'? Can't we have a real discussion around here?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Reminder to @pounded-pool: Let's maintain a respectful tone and focus on the topic at hand. Personal attacks are not constructive. Let's keep the conversation civil. Further violations may result in disciplinary action."

logs.name: yummy-dam
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @pounded-pool? You think you can just come at me with your condescending attitude and expect me to roll over? Newsflash: I'm not afraid of your threats. I'll keep calling out your BS and promoting facts, even if it makes you uncomfortable. And by the way, @moderator, I think you're being too soft on this guy. He's clearly not interested in having a respectful conversation. Maybe it's time to take a firmer stance?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Reminder to all users: Let's keep the conversation focused on the topic and maintain respect for each other. Personal attacks and threats will not be tolerated. Let's continue to engage in constructive dialogue. Further warnings may lead to disciplinary action."

logs.name: binding-stockade
logs.text: "Are you guys done with the drama? Can we please just stick to the topic at hand? I was trying to have a respectful conversation about art styles and you both are over here going back and forth. Can we please just focus?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "I agree with @binding-stockade. Let's refocus on the topic of art styles. @pounded-pool and @yummy-dam, please keep the conversation respectful and on topic. Further disruptions may result in disciplinary action."

logs.name: pounded-pool
logs.text: "Are you kidding me? I was just trying to share my opinion on the merits of abstract expressionism and you're already attacking me for it? @binding-stockade, I'm not the one who started this drama, you were the one who brought up the topic of art styles. Now you're acting like I'm the one who's being unreasonable. And by the way, what's with the condescending tone? It's not exactly constructive dialogue if you're talking down to me."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation constructive and respectful. @pounded-pool, @binding-stockade, let's focus on art styles. Further off-topic discussions will not be tolerated. Let's keep this a welcoming space for everyone."

logs.name: raw-armament
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, mods? Can't you see that @pounded-pool was just trying to contribute to the conversation? You're always so quick to jump on people who disagree with you. I think it's time to take a step back and actually listen to what people have to say, rather than just shutting them down. And by the way, @binding-stockade, your condescending tone is just as bad as @pounded-pool's initial post. Let's keep the discussion civil, but also allow for some real debate."
